name: Bar Crawls

interaction_intensity.label: Moderate-High Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: High momentum; the expectation to move with the group can feel pressured if you prefer a slower pace.

social_dynamics: Moving group energy, shared mission, collective decision-making.
solo_tips: Introduce yourself to the organizer immediately; they are your safety anchor.
Keep a digital copy of the route so you don't have to rely on following the crowd.

safety_factors.label: Moderate-High Risk
safety_factors.description: Moderate-high due to rapid venue transitions and the likelihood of getting separated from the main group.

physical_proximity: Frequent contact during transitions and while moving through narrow doors.
recommendations: Set a meeting point with a friend if you get separated
Carry a portable charger for long routes

name: Singles Mixer

interaction_intensity.label: Moderate Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: Icebreakers are the norm; there is a social expectation to engage, but it is structured rather than chaotic.

social_dynamics: Rotational seating or facilitated introductions; everyone is there with the same intent.
solo_tips: Arrive on time; the first 20 minutes are the easiest for facilitated entries.
The bar area is the best 'buffer' if you need a breather between rotations.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Low risk; usually a ticketed event with an organizer present to manage behavior.

physical_proximity: Controlled; choices between seated conversation and standing 'mingle zones'.
recommendations: Prepare a 30-second 'about me' to lower entry anxiety
Follow up with the organizer if you feel uncomfortable.

name: Structured Dance Lessons

interaction_intensity.label: Moderate Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: Focus is on learning; pressure is technical rather than purely social.

social_dynamics: Partner rotation is standard; you'll naturally interact with the whole room through the lesson.
solo_tips: No partner is needed; the rotation ensures you are never left out.
Wear smooth-soled shoes to make the physical interaction easier.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Low risk; professional environment with clear boundaries and instruction.

physical_proximity: High; requires direct hand/arm contact during the lesson.
recommendations: Focus on the steps first to reduce social 'noise'
Hydrate—it's more of a workout than it looks.
